_comment: Legacy allowlist for scripts/check-edge-function-auth.mjs.
Functions listed here are exempt from HARD-FAIL rules because they predate
the canonical auth pattern (requireServiceUser in _shared/service-auth.ts).
They are LATENTLY BROKEN on ES256 JWTs and should be migrated in batches.
Rules:
- unregistered_in_config: function folder has no [functions.X] block in
supabase/config.toml, so it inherits gateway verify_jwt=true. Gateway
rejects ES256 user tokens before the function runs.
- argless_get_user: function calls `supabase.auth.getUser()` with no arg.
supabase-js v2's local verifier rejects ES256.
To migrate a function OUT of this allowlist:
1. Add [functions.<name>] with explicit verify_jwt=false to config.toml.
2. Refactor to use requireServiceUser from _shared/service-auth.ts.
3. Remove the entry from this allowlist.
4. Deploy via Supabase MCP (preserves verify_jwt flag).
Any NEW function that appears in either category without being added here
will hard-fail the build.
Source of truth canonical example: supabase/functions/equipment-vision/index.ts
DEFERRED VARIANT COHORT — DO NOT MIGRATE WITH THE STANDARD PATTERN
These five functions are LEGITIMATELY broken on ES256 for real users, but
they cannot be migrated with a rote requireServiceUser swap. They need a
small auth-model design pass first. Tracked as deferred on purpose. See
the GitHub issue referenced in the commit history for full context.
1. Portal cohort (3) — serves EXTERNAL customer portal users, not
internal reps. Customers live in `portal_customers` (not `profiles`),
so requireServiceUser would 403 every portal customer with 'Profile
not found'. Migration plan: add a sibling helper
`_shared/portal-auth.ts::requirePortalUser()` that does GoTrue
validation + gates on `portal_customers.is_active`, returning the
portal_customer row as the workspace scope. ~40 lines. Then swap
the three portal functions to use it.
- portal-api
- portal-stripe
- portal-notification-refresh
2. OAuth callback cohort (2) — dual-flow endpoints: initiate requires
a rep JWT, callback has no JWT at all (uses signed-state cookie).
Migration plan: in the initiate branch, swap argless auth.getUser()
for requireServiceUser + admin/owner role narrowing. Leave the
callback branch alone (already stateless + cryptographically signed).
Gateway verify_jwt stays false so the callback can reach the fn
without an Authorization header.
- hubspot-oauth
- onedrive-oauth
